response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with ~13 years of experience, satisfying the bachelor's-degree-plus-8-years path on paper. However, the role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization. The resume shows essentially no hardware modeling, chip architecture, or AI-accelerator performance work; the closest adjacency is brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and high-throughput backend services. The domain mismatch is severe for a principal-level hardware IC role.
strengths: Long track record of high-performance, low-latency backend systems (millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure plus strong testing/TDD discipline
Meets the bachelor's + 8 years experience threshold
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or hardware-software co-design background
Principal-level hardware architecture role far outside the candidate's full-stack software domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator / DNN optimization background
